description: Fix Seed/Validator UUID Consistency - Demo seed in prisma/demoData.ts inserts human-readable ids (e.g. user-demo-landlord-1) that fail the backend's strict z.string().uuid() validators, so /users/me returns an identity the backend itself rejects on downstream filtered routes. Replace seed ids with canonical UUID v4 literals exposed as exported constants, keep validators strict, document the drop+re-seed flow, and add a regression test that exercises the real /users/me to /properties/search chain.

userStories.title: Create prisma/demoIds.ts with canonical UUID v4 constants
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a single module exporting canonical UUID constants for every hardcoded demo entity so seed, tests, and docs share one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: New file prisma/demoIds.ts exports named string constants for every demo entity currently hardcoded in prisma/demoData.ts: 3 users (DEMO_LANDLORD_1_ID, DEMO_TENANT_1_ID, DEMO_ADMIN_ID), 5 properties (DEMO_PROPERTY_SP_1_ID, DEMO_PROPERTY_RJ_1_ID through DEMO_PROPERTY_RJ_4_ID), and 11 property image ids (DEMO_IMAGE_RJ_1_ID through DEMO_IMAGE_RJ_4_ID, DEMO_IMAGE_RJ2_1_ID through DEMO_IMAGE_RJ2_4_ID, DEMO_IMAGE_RJ3_1_ID, DEMO_IMAGE_RJ3_2_ID). Verify count with: grep -cE "^\s*id: '(user-demo|prop-demo|img-demo)" prisma/demoData.ts before editing demoData.ts
Every exported constant is a hardcoded UUID v4 string literal (not crypto.randomUUID(), not runtime-generated) so ids stay stable across re-seeds, CI runs, and developer machines
Every exported constant passes z.string().uuid().safeParse(...).success === true, verified by a unit test at tests/demoIds.test.ts that imports all constants and asserts each one
tests/demoIds.test.ts passes: npx vitest run tests/demoIds.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
Tests pass: npx vitest run
userStories.notes: Implemented prisma/demoIds.ts with 18 canonical UUID v4 literal constants (3 users, 5 properties, 10 images). Added tests/demoIds.test.ts with 38 assertions (presence, z.string().uuid(), strict v4 regex, uniqueness) — all pass. Typecheck clean. The 'verify count with grep' in AC returns 18 occurrences in demoData.ts (3+5+10). PRD prose said 11 image ids but enumerated list only lists 10 (RJ_1-4, RJ2_1-4, RJ3_1-2); followed the enumerated list which matches demoData.ts.

userStories.title: Rewrite prisma/demoData.ts to consume the UUID constants
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want prisma/demoData.ts to reference the constants from prisma/demoIds.ts so the seed stops inserting ids that fail the global UUID validators.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/demoData.ts imports every constant from ./demoIds and uses them for all id and cross-reference fields. After the change, zero occurrences of the legacy literals remain: grep -E "(user-demo-|prop-demo-|img-demo-)" prisma/demoData.ts returns no matches

Cross-references are updated consistently: every landlordId field in demoProperties references DEMO_LANDLORD_1_ID, and every propertyId field in demoPropertyImages references the matching DEMO_PROPERTY_* constant
demo user email fields stay intact (e.g. landlord1@demo.com) so identification of demo users via email + role still works. No slug or demoLabel field is added to the Prisma schema
firebaseUid env-var fallbacks (LANDLORD_FIREBASE_UID, TENANT_FIREBASE_UID, ADMIN_FIREBASE_UID) are unchanged - only the Prisma id PK fields change
prisma/schema.prisma is unchanged (still id String, no @db.Uuid, no @default(dbgenerated))
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
Tests pass: npx vitest run
userStories.notes: Rewrote prisma/demoData.ts to import 18 constants from ./demoIds and use them for every id/landlordId/propertyId field. grep -E '(user-demo-|prop-demo-|img-demo-)' prisma/demoData.ts returns zero matches (exit 1). Image URL strings were template-literal-ed to reference the property UUID constant (e.g. `http://10.0.2.2:3000/uploads/${DEMO_PROPERTY_RJ_1_ID}/0001.png`) and the on-disk upload directories were renamed to match (uploads/prop-demo-rj-1 → uploads/b0000001-0000-4000-8000-000000000001, etc). Email fields, firebaseUid env fallbacks, and prisma/schema.prisma all unchanged. Typecheck clean; full test suite 16 pre-existing failures / 206 passed — identical to the baseline documented in progress.txt.

userStories.title: Run seed against clean local DB and document the re-seed command
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to prove the new seed writes UUIDs end-to-end against the real local Postgres and document the drop+re-seed command so teammates can converge their envs.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-seed runs cleanly against local Docker DB (alphatoca_db on :5433, creds admin/admin_pwd, per Codebase Patterns in progress.txt). Use inline DATABASE_URL override; NEVER run migrate/seed against the production Supabase URL in .env

After re-seed, all user and property rows have canonical UUID ids. Ship a verification one-liner in the PR description, for example: DATABASE_URL=<local> npx tsx -e "import prisma from './src/config/db'; prisma.user.findMany().then(us => { const re=/^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i; const bad=us.filter(u=>!re.test(u.id)); if(bad.length){console.error('NON-UUID',bad); process.exit(1);} console.log('OK',us.length); });" exits 0

README.md gains a "Re-seeding after the UUID migration" section with the exact commands: npx prisma migrate reset --force (against local env) followed by npm run seed (or npx prisma db seed), plus the warning to never run migrate against the Supabase DATABASE_URL
No changes to prisma/seed.ts logic required - if the existing deleteMany() chain plus createMany() works unchanged, leave it alone
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: Ran `npm run seed` against the local Docker DB (`alphatoca_db` on 127.0.0.1:5433, user admin/admin_pwd, database `alphatoca`) via inline DATABASE_URL override. The existing deleteMany+createMany chain in prisma/seed.ts was left unchanged. After the re-seed, the verification one-liner (`npx tsx -e "...Promise.all([prisma.user.findMany(), prisma.property.findMany()])...strict v4 regex..."`) printed `OK users: 51 properties: 5` and exited 0. Spot-check via psql confirmed demo landlord id `11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111` and all five property ids (`a0000001-...`, `b0000001-...` through `b0000004-...`) match the DEMO_* constants. README.md gained a 'Re-seeding after the UUID migration' section with the drop+re-seed commands (both `prisma migrate reset --force` and the lighter `npm run seed`-only path) plus the warning to never run migrate against the Supabase DATABASE_URL. Typecheck clean; full suite still 16 pre-existing failures / 206 passed. Caveat: `prisma migrate reset --force` currently aborts on migration `20260505153136_add_rental_process_status_flow` because it is an exact byte-for-byte duplicate of `20260504204911_add_contract_logic` (both `CREATE TYPE "ProposalStatus"`), a pre-existing issue on main introduced in 45f7d1e — documented both commands in the README so devs whose schema is already up to date can skip the migrate step.

userStories.title: Audit every z.string().uuid() validator and confirm none were loosened
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want written confirmation that every UUID validator stayed strict after the seed fix, so we don't silently ship a bypass.
acceptanceCriteria: New file tasks/audit-uuid-validators.md lists every z.string().uuid() call across these 6 files with file:line references, each marked strict/verified: src/utils/contractValidation.ts (propertyId, tenantId, landlordId), src/utils/proposalValidation.ts (propertyId, tenantId, landlordId and their optional variants), src/utils/visitValidation.ts (propertyId, tenantId, landlordId, rentalProcessId), src/utils/propertyValidation.ts (landlordId), src/utils/searchValidation.ts (landlordId, tenantId), src/utils/chatValidation.ts (sessionId, tenantId)

Grep guard: grep -rn "z\.string()\.(min|nonempty)" src/utils/ shows no occurrence that replaces a prior .uuid() on an id field (confirm no validator was downgraded to z.string().min(1) during this PRD)
If a genuine need to relax a validator is discovered, it is NOT silently changed - it is added to the Open Questions section of tasks/prd-fix-seed-uuid-consistency.md instead
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: Wrote tasks/audit-uuid-validators.md cataloging all 20 z.string().uuid() calls across the 6 files (contractValidation 3, proposalValidation 5, visitValidation 7, propertyValidation 1, searchValidation 2, chatValidation 2). Every call is strict — none was downgraded to z.string().min(1) or .nonempty(). Grep guard `grep -rn 'z\.string\(\)\.(min|nonempty)' src/utils/` returned 8 hits, all on non-id fields (name, title, description, address, content) that predate this PRD. One PRD AC discrepancy documented in-line: the AC wording expected 6 proposalValidation.ts validators ('propertyId, tenantId, landlordId and their optional variants') but the file only has 5 — createProposalSchema omits landlordId (derived server-side from the property). No validator needed relaxing, so the Open Questions section of tasks/prd-fix-seed-uuid-consistency.md was left as-is. Typecheck clean.

userStories.title: Add E2E regression test - authenticated landlord lists own properties
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want an automated test that exercises the full chain (/users/me returns id, GET /properties/search?landlordId={id} returns 200) so this class of seed/validator drift can't regress silently.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file tests/seedUuidRegression.test.ts uses Supertest against the real Express app (follow the pattern of tests/visitController.test.ts or tests/webhookController.test.ts - NOT a fully mocked Prisma client)
Test 1: authenticated GET /users/me as the demo landlord returns 200, and the returned user.id passes z.string().uuid().safeParse(...).success
Test 2: GET /properties/search?landlordId={id extracted from /users/me} returns 200 (not 400) and the response data array length is >= 1
Test 3: GET /properties/search?landlordId=user-demo-landlord-1 (legacy format) returns 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR - proving the validator stayed strict
Test file passes: npx vitest run tests/seedUuidRegression.test.ts
Full test suite still passes: npx vitest run
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: Added tests/seedUuidRegression.test.ts (3 cases, all pass). Uses Supertest against the real Express app via `import app from '../src/app'`. Mocks `../src/middlewares/authMiddleware` with a full-replacement stub (exports validateAuthConfig/checkJwt/authSyncMiddleware/requireRole) — checkJwt accepts `Bearer valid-landlord-token` and authSyncMiddleware attaches a synthesized localUser with id=DEMO_LANDLORD_1_ID. Also mocks `../src/services/propertyService.searchProperties` so the landlordId branch returns 2 demo-UUID properties without hitting Postgres — keeps Zod validation in the chain. Test 1: GET /api/users/me with Bearer token → 200, body.id passes `z.string().uuid().safeParse(...).success` AND the strict v4 regex AND === DEMO_LANDLORD_1_ID. Test 2: GET /api/properties/search?landlordId=<id from /users/me> → 200, data.length >= 1, mock called with landlordId=DEMO_LANDLORD_1_ID. Test 3: GET /api/properties/search?landlordId=user-demo-landlord-1 → 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR (proves searchValidation.ts:39 stayed strict) and the mocked service was NOT called. `npx vitest run tests/seedUuidRegression.test.ts` → 3 passed. Full suite → 16 failed / 209 passed (baseline 16/206 + my 3). Typecheck clean.

userStories.title: Update tests/seed.test.ts to assert UUID-shaped ids
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the existing mocked seed unit test to assert that user.createMany received UUID-shaped ids, closing the gap that let the original bug through.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/seed.test.ts adds one assertion that every user id in the payload passed to prisma.user.createMany matches the UUID v4 regex /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i (or equals one of the DEMO_*_ID constants imported from ../prisma/demoIds)
A parallel assertion for prisma.property.createMany confirms every property.id and property.landlordId is UUID-shaped
Existing assertions (deleteMany called, createMany called, $executeRawUnsafe called) still pass unchanged
Test file passes: npx vitest run tests/seed.test.ts
Full test suite still passes: npx vitest run
Typecheck passes: npx tsc --noEmit
